title: DB20-G Interface Box — Hardware Build Guide

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The DB20-G Interface Box connects your Android phone to the Radioddity DB20-G GMRS radio via a single USB-C cable. It provides serial control (programming, channel management) and audio I/O (transmit/receive voice) through a custom PCB.
items: USB-C phone connection via FE1.1s USB hub
CP2102N USB-to-UART for serial programming at 9600 baud
CM108AH USB audio codec for voice TX/RX
PTT control via 2N2222A transistor (RTS) or G5V-1 relay (DTR)
RJ-45 handset pass-through (use radio with hand mic AND phone simultaneously)
3.5mm TRS jack for rear data/programming port
4 status LEDs: Power (green), PTT (red), Audio (yellow), Serial (blue)

sections.title: Required Tools
items: Hot air station (optional, for QFN rework)
Magnifying glass or loupe (for SMD inspection)
Isopropyl alcohol and brush (for flux cleanup)
3D printer (for enclosure) or order from service

subsections.title: RJ-45 — Radio Handset Port (J2/J3)
table: Tapped for CM108AH TX audio output
Active LOW — tapped for Q1 collector / K1 relay NO
Tapped for CM108AH RX audio input

steps.text: Solder U4 (AMS1117-3.3) — orient dot to pin 1
Solder C7 (10µF) input cap near U4 IN pin
Solder C8 (22µF) output cap near U4 OUT pin
Solder F1 (500mA polyfuse) in VBUS path
Solder D2 (1N5819 Schottky) for reverse polarity protection
TEST: Apply 5V to F1 input. Verify 3.3V on U4 OUT pin.
Solder U1 (FE1.1s) — align pin 1 dot carefully
Solder Y1 (12MHz crystal) to XI/XO pins
Solder C11, C12 (22pF) crystal load caps
Solder C1 (100nF) and C2 (10µF) bypass caps
Solder R11 (4.7kΩ) and R12 (1.5kΩ) USB pull-ups
Solder J1 (USB-C receptacle) — inspect all pins under magnification
Connect GANGED (pin 9) and SELF_PWR (pin 10) to GND
TEST: Connect to phone. USB hub should enumerate (check dmesg/logcat).
Solder U2 (CP2102N QFN-28) — use flux, inspect bridges carefully
Solder C3 (100nF) and C4 (10µF) bypass caps
Connect U2 RST pin to 3V3 with 10kΩ pull-up
Solder J4 (3.5mm TRS jack)
TEST: Connect to phone. CP2102 should appear as /dev/ttyUSB0 in app.
Solder U3 (CM108AH SSOP-28)
Solder C5 (100nF) and C6 (10µF) bypass caps
Solder RX audio path: C9 (1µF) → R3 (10kΩ) → R4 (1kΩ) divider
Solder TX audio path: C10 (1µF) → R5 (10kΩ) → R6 (1kΩ) divider
TEST: CM108 should appear as USB audio device. Play a tone through the app.
Solder R1 (10kΩ) base resistor for Q1
Solder Q1 (2N2222A) — flat side faces silkscreen outline
Solder R2 (10kΩ) base resistor for Q2
Solder Q2 (2N2222A) — flat side faces silkscreen outline
Solder K1 (G5V-1-DC5 relay)
Solder D1 (1N4148) flyback diode across K1 coil — cathode band to +5V
TEST: Toggle RTS in app. Q1 collector should pull PTT low. Verify with multimeter.
Solder R7 (330Ω) and LED1 (Green — Power)
Solder R8 (330Ω) and LED2 (Red — PTT)
Solder R9 (330Ω) and LED3 (Yellow — Audio)
Solder R10 (330Ω) and LED4 (Blue — Serial)
Solder J2 (RJ-45 — Radio side)
Solder J3 (RJ-45 — Handset pass-through)
Wire pass-through: J2 pins 1,3,6,7,8 → corresponding J3 pins
Wire tapped lines: J2 pin 2→TX audio, pin 4→PTT, pin 5→RX audio
TEST: Power LED should light. Connect radio handset to verify pass-through.
Clean flux residue with isopropyl alcohol
Inspect all solder joints under magnification
Mount PCB in 3D-printed enclosure with M3 screws
Verify LED alignment through enclosure light pipes
Full system test: connect phone, radio, and handset simultaneously
Test serial download/upload from this app
Test PTT key/unkey from this app
Test audio receive (radio → phone)
Test audio transmit (phone → radio)
Test handset pass-through (mic, PTT, UP, DOWN buttons)

solutions: Verify USB-C cable supports data (not charge-only)
Check F1 polyfuse — measure continuity
Verify 5V present on VBUS pin of J1
Check FE1.1s crystal oscillation — probe XO with scope
Ensure phone supports USB OTG (check in Settings → About → USB)

issues.problem: Serial port not found in app
solutions: Check CP2102N solder joints (especially QFN ground pad)
Verify 3.3V on U2 VDD pin
Check USB D+/D− connections from hub port 1 to CP2102N
Try different baud rate in app settings (should be 9600)
Verify J4 TRS jack connections (Tip=TX, Ring=RX, Sleeve=GND)
Check CM108AH solder joints
Verify RX path: J2 pin 5 → C9 → R3/R4 divider → U3 MIC_IN
Measure voltage at R3/R4 junction — should be ~0.1× speaker voltage
Check coupling cap C9 — must not be shorted
Verify CM108AH appears as USB audio device in Android settings
Verify RTS signal: measure U2 pin 23 — should toggle 0V/3.3V
Check R1→Q1 base — should see ~0.33mA when RTS high
Measure Q1 collector to emitter — should be <0.2V when active
Verify J2 pin 4 is connected to Q1 collector
Check that radio PTT is active-LOW (most GMRS radios are)
Try DTR/relay mode: toggle PTT mode in app Settings
Add ferrite bead on AVCC power line to CM108AH
Check ground connections — ensure single-point grounding
Separate audio ground pour from digital ground on PCB
Add 100pF caps across R3 and R5 (audio filter)
Move audio traces away from switching/digital lines
Check all 8 RJ-45 pin connections J2 ↔ J3
Verify continuity on each pass-through wire
Ensure PTT tap doesn't load the handset PTT line excessively
Check that Q1 collector is high-impedance when not keyed

sections.title: Bill of Materials Summary
sections.content: Estimated cost: $17–$20 (excluding cables). Full BOM with LCSC part numbers is in the hardware/BOM.csv file for direct JLCPCB ordering.
items: LED1–LED4: Green, Red, Yellow, Blue (0805 SMD)

sections.title: Circuit Block Diagram
sections.diagram:
Phone ──USB-C──► [F1 Fuse] ──► [FE1.1s Hub]
                                    │
                       ┌────────────┴────────────┐
                       ▼                         ▼
                  [CP2102N]                 [CM108AH]
                   Serial                    Audio
                       │                         │
              ┌────────┴────┐          ┌─────────┴────────┐
              ▼             ▼          ▼                  ▼
          [J4 TRS]    [PTT Drive]  [RX Atten]       [TX Atten]
          Data Port    Q1/Q2/K1    10:1 divider     10:1 divider
              │             │          │                  │
              ▼             ▼          ▼                  ▼
          Radio Rear    Radio PTT   Radio SPK→Phone   Phone→Radio MIC
          Data Port     (J2 pin4)   (J2 pin5)        (J2 pin2)

              ◄──── RJ-45 Pass-through (J2 ↔ J3) ────►
              Radio                              Handset
